title: Any recommendations on getting a hardcopy of EoE
display_title: Any recommendations on getting a hardcopy of EoE
wikitext:
[]: Any recommendations on getting a hardcopy of EoE?             Quote Post by LeCapEtan » Sat Aug 21, 2010 11:15 pm Ok, I recently purchased a brand new platinum pack of the series and it's great and fantastic and everything. However, now I obviously want to get EoE. I mostly want to get a hardcopy (aka, the actual DVD or some way to get a computer file version to work through a DVD) because my best friend (who is computer illiterate and only owns a 6 year old Mac laptop that can't play my .mkv version of EoE) won't/can't watch the series and movie unless he can put it on his TV. This is a very round-a-bout way of asking 3 things. 1) I would much prefer a legit copy, however I am not forking over $80+ at this time for the movie. How safe do you think buying used from Amazon is? I mean, I know Amazon is usually VERY reliable with used things, but with this being an out of print item, and with most of the sellers being generally new sellers, do you think it's worth taking the risk? 2) If I end up not wanting to buy used right now, is there a way to convert or burn my mkv file onto a DVD that a player might be able to read? Or maybe a way to get my Xbox 360 to play it? (again, hooking my friends mac up to his TV won't help) 3) Open suggestions. Anything I haven't thought of, such as reliable sites I wouldn't know about, etc... Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 21, 2010 11:25 pm If you're buying used from Amazon just check feedback; the same as on eBay. Other than that, you could just burn a file of it onto a DVD-R and it should work fine. An .ISO file would be best if you could find one, though there actually might be some higher quality versions out there outside the DVD, which is pretty bad.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sat Aug 21, 2010 11:40 pm This here's a shocker: a bad DVD release for a low price. Definitely not recommended to get it yet. (how is it a bad release? Non-anamorphic widescreen, blurry picture)

[]:             Quote Post by LeCapEtan » Sat Aug 21, 2010 11:46 pm Wow, I guess I'll avoid the DVD. Like I said, the file I have (mkv file, 1.36 Gigs, got it from a friend so I don't know the original source he "got" it from) is absolutely gorgeous, so I guess I'll experiment with converting it to an iso file (or if I get lazy I'll look for an already made one).

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Aug 22, 2010 12:21 am No need to convert it to an .ISO if the quality is already fine.

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Sun Aug 22, 2010 7:56 am LeCapEtan wrote: Wow, I guess I'll avoid the DVD. Like I said, the file I have (mkv file, 1.36 Gigs, got it from a friend so I don't know the original source he "got" it from) is absolutely gorgeous, so I guess I'll experiment with converting it to an iso file (or if I get lazy I'll look for an already made one).

[]:             Quote Post by LeCapEtan » Sun Aug 22, 2010 9:57 am Hawq wrote: If you want to turn that into a DVD then I recommend trying ConvertXtoDVD its easy to use & always gives me good results, simple to add extra audio tracks (of course if the mkv has more than one they'll be listed automatically) & subtitles (again if the file has em in they will be added on automatically) etc.. as well

[]:             Quote Post by SpearofLonginus » Tue Aug 24, 2010 6:57 pm This is kinda OT, but I have a question regarding the US release of End of Evangelion. If it matters I have the box set vers that came with Death and Rebirth too. Am I right to understand we are missing a live action scene and were jipped on audio quality? ^^; I swear I remember the live action scene in the movie towards the end and I could kinda care less about audio.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:42 pm SpearofLonginus wrote: Am I right to understand we are missing a live action scene and were jipped on audio quality? ^^; I swear I remember the live action scene in the movie towards the end and I could kinda care less about audio.

[]:             Quote Post by scarmullet » Tue Aug 24, 2010 7:57 pm You convert the mkv file to DVD compliant files, it involves a lot of software and it takes a while [url]http://forum.videohelp.com/threads/192294-HOW-TO-CONVERT-AVI-OGM-or-MKV-to-DVD[/url] video help is a great resource for software and the like

[]:             Quote Post by SpearofLonginus » Tue Aug 24, 2010 8:00 pm drinian wrote: The US release of EoE is the same as the original Japanese DVD release, both in terms of content and (low) a/v quality. Unfortunately, the US never got the Renewal release that came out in Japan some years ago. I don't know if any other DVD regions ever got it, but YesAsia doesn't show anything available.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Tue Aug 24, 2010 8:21 pm I think that I have the same MKV file of EOE as you. The only problem is that the subtitles aren't 100% literal.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Aug 24, 2010 9:22 pm SpearofLonginus wrote: Whats the difference between the original DVD release and the Renewal? (content wise, not specs)

[]:             Quote Post by Zenithfleet » Mon Sep 06, 2010 6:22 am drinian wrote: The film itself is identical to the point where the ZX "fansub" is essentially the subtitles and dub track from the US Manga release, sync'd to the new video and audio from the Renewal release.
